entry.title: Re: Updating the ShortText of an Inspection Lot

entry.title: Key Figure calculation, formula to check for ONLY values (NOT NULL) in attribute on Planning Object.
entry.content:
I am trying to configure a keyfigure with checks such as: 

Condition 1: At customer product and shipto level, XYZPROP key figure has 0 value in for CURRENT TO 13 PERIODS- for which I created an helper key figure. with IBP_DYNAMIC_RAGGR("ABC@MTHPRODLOCCUSTWE",''SUM'', 0, 13, ''CURRENTFUTURE'')  and Condition2: If attribute SHIPFROMDEFAULTLOC at customer product loc and shipto level is NOT blank, then check the SHIPFROMDEFAULTLOC value is equal to locid then assign ZWEPROPSTAT with the ZWEADJUSTEDACTUALSQTY in the future buckets to that level record.

What formula in the key figure calculation would work to check for planning objects that has values (NOT NULL) in attribute on Planning Object? Per my understanding, we can only use static value. Is that true?

entry.title: Questions Regarding Cost Centers and Allocation Rules in SAP
entry.content:
Good afternoon,

I would like to ask some questions related to cost centers, as we identified certain issues upon activating them that have raised some doubts.

Context:
Currently, we have automatic invoicing enabled in SAP, which means that allocation rules are assigned automatically and cannot be manually modified.

When configuring the allocation rules with a start date of 01/01/2025, we did not consider that there would still be re-invoicing and pending records from 2024. This caused errors when attempting to process those records. To address the issue, we initially:

  1. Unassigned all allocation rules from the accounts.
  2. Disabled the cost centers.

However, this did not resolve the problem, as the errors persisted. Eventually, we re-enabled the allocation rules, as the records are linked to them. This approach successfully resolved the observed errors.

Questions:

  1. From what date should the allocation rules be initiated to avoid errors related to cancellations and records from a previous fiscal year?
  2. Since it is not possible to edit the start date of the existing rules, would it be advisable to create new rules with dates prior to 2025?
  3. What recommendations can you provide to ensure a successful implementation, avoiding errors in fiscal year closures, journal entries, sales, and related processes?

I appreciate your guidance in advance and remain available for further clarification.

Best regards,

entry.title: Flexible Workflows in SAP Purchase Requisition
entry.content:
SAP Workflow Configuration for Purchase Requisitions

 1.  Perform Customizing for the Flexible Workflow for Purchase Requisitions

 1.1 MANDATORY – Check Automatic Workflow Customizing

Ensure that the automatic Customizing is active for the workflow functionality -

Configuration: SPRO –> IMG –> ABAP Platform –> Application Server–>Business Management > SAP Business Workflow > Maintain Standard Settings

Tcode: SWU3

Depending on whether you have previously used a workflow functionality, proceed as follows:

  • If you have previously used a workflow functionality (either SAP Business Workflow or flexible workflow), check whether all Customizing steps listed have a green checkmark. If not, click Redo Automatic Customizing or select one of the activities and click Execute Activity to make the required settings.

  • If you have not used any workflow so far, click Automatic Workflow Customizing to make the settings.

1.2 MANDATORY – Activate Flexible Workflow Scenarios

 Activate the workflow scenarios for a type of business document, e.g, purchase requisition.

Configuration: SPRO > IMG > ABAP Platform > Application Server > Business Management > SAP Business Workflow > Flexible Workflow > Scenario Activation

Old Scenarios – These scenarios will not be enhanced in future releases and SAP recommends using the new scenarios.

Workflow Scenario ID

Workflow Name

Standard Task ID

Task Name

WS00800157

Overall Release of Purchase Requisition

TS00800547

Overall release of PR

WS00800173

Release of Purchase Requisition Item

TS00800548

Release of PR Item

New Scenarios –

Workflow Scenario ID

Workflow Name

Standard Task ID

Task Name

WS02000471

Release of Purchase Requisition Item

TS02000714

Release of PR Item

WS02000458

Overall Release of Purchase Requisition

TS02000702

Overall release of Purchase Requisition

Depending on the business requirement for item wise release or header wise release, the scenario needs to be activated, here we have activated the Item wise Release of Purchase Requisitions with the new scenario Id’s.

1.3 MANDATORY – Define Step Names and Decision options in My Inbox

 Check step names and decision options to be used in the app My Inbox

 Configuration: SPRO > IMG > ABAP Platform > SAP Gateway Service Enablement > Content > Workflow Settings > Maintain Task Names and Decision Options.

With New Scenario Id’s -

 

Workflow Scenario ID

Step ID

Step Description

WS02000458

0000000019

Overall Release of Purchase Requisition

WS02000471

0000000020

Release Purchase Requisition Item

With Old Scenario ID’s -  

Workflow Scenario ID

Step ID

Step Description

WS00800157

0000000010

Overall Release of Purchase Requisition

WS00800157

0000000049

Overall Release of Purchase Requisition

WS00800173

0000000010

Release Purchase Requisition Item

WS00800173

0000000032

Release Purchase Requisition Item

 

1.4 MANDATORY- Define Visualization Metadata for My Inbox (for Purchase Requisitions)

When you use the flexible workflow for the approval of procurement documents, you have to ensure that the app My Inbox is available and set up for your approvers.

To do this, you have to enter the data listed in below configuration

Configuration: Materials Management > Purchasing > Purchase Requisition > Flexible Workflow for Purchase Requisitions > Client-Dependent Configuration.

As the worklist client, select SAPUI5 My Inbox or My Inbox, Select the Task tab and choose Create New Line

For the workflow for the overall release of regular purchase requisitions (workflow scenario ID WS02000458 and task ID TS02000702, with new scenario ID’s -

Parameter Name

Visualization Parameter Value

SCHEME

Sapui5

COMPONENT_NAME

cross.fnd.fiori.inbox.annotationBasedTaskUI

QUERY_PARAM00

service=/sap/opu/odata/SAP/C_PURREQUISITION_FS_SRV

QUERY_PARAM01

entity=/C_PurRequisitionFs(PurchaseRequisition='{OBJKEY}')

QUERY_PARAM02

annotations=/sap/opu/odata/IWFND/CATALOGSERVICE;v=2/Annotations(TechnicalName='C_PURREQUISITION_FS_ANNO_MDL',Version='0001')/$value

For the workflow for the release of regular purchase requisition items (workflow scenario ID WS02000471 and task ID TS02000714😞

Parameter Name

Visualization Parameter Value

SCHEME

Sapui5

COMPONENT_NAME

cross.fnd.fiori.inbox.annotationBasedTaskUI

QUERY_PARAM00

service=/sap/opu/odata/sap/C_PURREQUISITIONITEM_FS_SRV

QUERY_PARAM01

entity=/C_PurRequisitionItemFs(PurchaseRequisition='{&_WI_OBJECT_ID.MS_PUR_REQ_ITEM.PURCHASEREQUISITION&}',PurchaseRequisitionItem='{&_WI_OBJECT_ID.MS_PUR_REQ_ITEM.PURCHASEREQUISITIONITEM&}')

QUERY_PARAM02

annotations=/sap/opu/odata/IWFND/CATALOGSERVICE;v=2/Annotations(TechnicalName='C_PURREQUISITIONITEM_FS_ANNO_MDL',Version='0001')/$value

For the old workflow for the overall release of regular purchase requisitions (workflow scenario ID WS00800157 and task ID TS00800547😞

 

Parameter Name

Visualization Parameter Value

SCHEME

Sapui5

COMPONENT_NAME

cross.fnd.fiori.inbox.annotationBasedTaskUI

QUERY_PARAM00

service=/sap/opu/odata/sap/C_PURREQUISITION_FS_SRV

QUERY_PARAM01

entity=/C_PurRequisitionFs(PurchaseRequisition='{&_WI_Object_ID.Number&}')

QUERY_PARAM02

annotations=/sap/opu/odata/IWFND/CATALOGSERVICE;v=2/Annotations(TechnicalName='C_PURREQUISITION_FS_ANNO_MDL',Version='0001')/$value

For the old workflow for the release of regular purchase requisition items (workflow scenario ID WS00800173 and task ID TS00800548).

 

Parameter Name

Visualization Parameter Value

SCHEME

Sapui5

COMPONENT_NAME

cross.fnd.fiori.inbox.annotationBasedTaskUI

QUERY_PARAM00

service=/sap/opu/odata/sap/C_PURREQUISITIONITEM_FS_SRV

QUERY_PARAM01

entity=/C_PurRequisitionItemFs(PurchaseRequisition='{&_WI_Object_ID.Number&}',PurchaseRequisitionItem='{&_WI_Object_ID.Item&}')

QUERY_PARAM02

annotations=/sap/opu/odata/IWFND/CATALOGSERVICE;v=2/Annotations(TechnicalName='C_PURREQUISITIONITEM_FS_ANNO_MDL',Version='0001')/$value

1.5 MANDATORY- Deactivate Event type linkages for SAP Business Workflows.

Deactivate workflows based on old workflow scenarios, As a rule, activating flexible workflow scenarios (see above Activate flexible workflow scenarios) is sufficient to override old workflow scenarios. To be on the safe side, you can additionally deactivate any old workflow scenarios you may have used previously.

1.6 MANDATORY- Generalize tasks and activate event type linkages for flexible workflow.

We have to ensure that the system events are linked to your workflow scenarios, and you have to define the potential agents of workflow tasks to clearly define the organizational responsibility for processing work items. Perform the Customizing steps described below.

Configuration: SPRO > IMG > ABAP Platform > Application Server > Business Management > SAP Business Workflow > Perform task-specific Customizing.

To generalize your workflow tasks, navigate to the required SAP application component, for example MM-PUR-REQ - Purchase Requisitions and proceed as follows:

  1. Click on Assign Agents
  2. Select a standard task for your business object. (The IDs of tasks start with TS). See Flexible Workflow in Sourcing and Procurement: Scenarios and Tasks Overview. Do not select a background task.
  3. Click Attributes and select General Task.
  4. Leave the other settings unchanged and click Transfer

  1. Click Activate Event Linking
  2. Select a workflow scenario for your business object. (The IDs of workflow scenarios start with WS).
  3. Expand the hierarchy to display the relevant event.
  4. Activate the linkage for the listed event.

1.7 MANDATORY- Register gateway service for value helps (BASIS and ABAP required).

We have to register an OData service to enable the value help in the flexible workflow.

In transaction Activate and Maintain Services /n/IWFND/MAINT_SERVICE)

Configuration Path: SPRO > ABAP Platform > SAP Gateway > OData Channel > Administration  General Settings > Activate and Maintain Services

1.8 Activate Flexible Workflow for Purchase Requisitions

Scenario Based for Itemwise release & OvRel for Overall release -

2. Master Data Setup:

2.1 HCM creates a Personnel No for the SAP User ID with infoset “Communication” and assign the SAP user ID to the Personnel Number.

2.2 Create a “Person” BP for the SAP user ID and assign the Personnel No. from HCM into the identification type of the BP.

2.3 Run HR BP sync in SE38 for the Personnel No created from HCM.  –

 /SHCM/RH_SYNC_BUPA_EMPL_SINGLE

BAPI “MM_PUR_S4_PR_WFL_RESTART “to be activated in case of version management is active and changes are made to the PO after approval to retrigger the SAP workflow.

3. Customizations to be done in FIORI

3.1 Activate APP – Manage Workflows for Purchase Requisitions.

3.2 In the Fiori APP – manage workflows for Purchase Requisition Items, select Release of Purchase Requisition Item and click on create.

3.3 Enter the Workflow name -

3.4 Select the Start Conditions - Based upon the Organizations requirement set the Start Conditions.

To add custom new preconditions in Flexible Workflow SAP has given a BADI SWF_WORKFLOW_CONDITION_DEF to enhance and add your required fields in the preconditions tab. Along with this we also need to add evaluation logic for these custom defined pre-conditions in BADI SWF_WORKFLOW_CONDITION_EVAL.

BAdI SWF_WORKFLOW_CONDITION_DEF allows you to create the fields for key and value to add in the start and step conditions and SWF_WORKFLOW_CONDITION_EVAL helps in valuating the value part of these fields.

3.5 Create Steps -

Select the Step Type, Assignment By, and step to be completed by, enter the assignment by and the corresponding usernames.

Select the Action required incase the PR Item is rejected in the Exception Handling.

3.6 Save the Workflow and once saved activate the workflow

4. Transaction & Execution -

Create Purchase Requisition

Open the PR in the changed mode to get the Approval details in the line item.

Goto My Inbox in Fiori –

Approve PR -

Approved status updated in PR -

Reject PR from my Inbox –

PR created and in Ready status –

After Rejection PR comes to ready state -

Thank you.

Regards,

Sagnik Chakravarty. 

 

References –

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/af9ef57f504840d2b81be8667206d485/91ab576eaf58475f8934d2dcef16edb3.html?version=1909.000

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/af9ef57f504840d2b81be8667206d485/91c0680c15c746e4b5fd697aca13e2ac.html?version=1909.000

entry.title: Program RPCIPE00 looks like affect BW Datasource 0HR_PY_PP_1
entry.content:
Dear all, are you aware of any issue related to SAP BW datasource 0HR_PY_PP_1  when  HR team is using program RPCIPE00 in S4/HANA instead of RPCIPE00_OLD? I have opened a case within SAP. Just wondering if you needed to change any configuration when start using RPCIPE00.

We are seeing datasource 0HR_PY_PP_1, for Retroactive Run Numbers, it is not bringing the "P" record (Indicator: Status of Record) for some wage types. In BW query then, some wage types are showing duplicated, because we were expecting a positive amount and a negative amount for retroactive run, which is not happening anymore in some cases.

Thanks,

Marcelo

entry.title: material ledger type（ECC TO S4）
entry.content: ECC with data upgrade S4, A factory has activated the material ledger, and after the upgrade, the corresponding material ledger type for A factory is 9000; B factory has not activated the material ledger. After the upgrade, the corresponding material ledger type for B factory is 9200 9000：10 Company code currency； 9200：10 Company code currency + 20 Controlling area currency Company code currency = CNY； Company code currency = CNY Question: Why is the material ledger type corresponding to Factory A not 9200?

entry.title: Sell from Subcontractor - With Shipment Processing Part 1
entry.content:
Business Background

The sell-from-subcontractor cycle is a strategic solution that can be employed by companies to address manufacturing capacity constraints while ensuring efficient fulfilment of customer demands. In this process, raw materials are sent to a subcontractor, who leverages their facilities and expertise to manufacture finished goods in accordance with the company’s specifications. These goods are then maintained in customer consignment stock under the supplier’s ownership, providing flexibility and readiness for customer orders. When a customer demand arises, the company strategically authorizes the subcontractor to ship the finished products directly to the customer, bypassing internal logistics and minimizing lead times. This streamlined approach not only alleviates manufacturing bottlenecks but also enhances operational efficiency, reduces costs, and ensures prompt delivery. Leveraging SAP functionalities, such as subcontracting purchase orders, consignment stock management, and delivery processes, this cycle provides end-to-end visibility and control, enabling companies to maintain high levels of customer satisfaction and operational excellence. Below is the value chain diagram of the scenario.

Table of Content

    1. Material Master
    2. Business Partner
    3. Supplier Info Record
    4. Create Sales Order Subcontractor Fill-up
    5. Create Purchase Order
    6. Create Outbound Delivery - Raw Materials
    7. Create Shipment & Shipment Completion (Goods Issue)
    8. Post Goods Movement - Place in Storage
    9. Post Goods Movement - Goods Receipt
    10. Create Outbound Delivery - Customer Consignment
    11. Create Supplier Invoice & Outgoing Payment
    12. Bonus
    13. Resources

Visit https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-members/sell-from-subcontractor-with-shipment-processing-part-2/ba-p/13978351 for the Configuration Guide.

Master Data

The master data section will focus on maintaining key elements to enable the sell-from-subcontractor process effectively. This includes updating the material master by adding valuation types to support split valuation and defining special procurement keys to support subcontracting processes. Furthermore, the creation of business partner records will be discussed, emphasizing the integration of supplier and customer roles to streamline interactions across procurement and sales processes. Additionally, the supplier info record setup will be covered, establishing a link between materials and subcontractors while detailing procurement-specific attributes such as pricing, lead times, and subcontracting parameters. These setups ensure accurate and integrated master data, providing a solid foundation and automation throughout the sell-from-subcontractor cycle.

1. Material Master

1. First, for the finished goods, go to MRP 2 view in the material master and maintain procurement type X. The procurement key indicates that this material can be used for inhouse production. Therefore, being able to use it in production process as well as being able to use it in subcontracting scenario. Add special procurement 30. Special procurement is used to get the subcontracting service cost in the sales order cost estimate.

2. In the same view, add stock determination group Z001 that is maintained and mentioned in Stock Determination configuration section. The stock determination group will be used in the automatic determination of the internal finished goods valuation type.

3. In MRP 4, activate the version indicator by choosing production version, as this needs to be activated to be used in BOM.

4. In Accounting 1 view, add the valuation category created in the Split Valuation configuration section.

5. Now, after saving Accounting 1 view, maintain the standard price for the internal and external valuation types. Re-enter the Accounting 1 view and add the internal manufacturing valuation type first then repeat for the external subcontracting valuation type.

a. In this scenario, you can maintain a separate valuation class for the sales order stock. Note, this will affect G/L account of the inventory finished goods used in the cycle. This will be discussed further in the processes.

6. For the raw material, go to the MRP 4 view in the material master and for the Individual/Coll.Req maintain 2 (Collective Requirements Only). By doing this, you would be able to issue the raw materials from company's own stock.

2. Business Partner

1. Create supplier business partner and extend it to customer. Note that Maintain Business Partner application was used in this scenario as there are fields that do not exist in Manage Business Partner and that is because of the following SAP note 2865228.

2. Maintain the normal data for the customer, as of general information, customer pricing procedure, but most importantly the delivering plant as it is used in the determination of delivery type that is mentioned in Subcontracting Delivery configuration section.

3. Maintain the normal data for the FI supplier role and supplier. However, the Returns supplier flag needs to be raised in order to enable the creation of outbound delivery for raw materials.

a. You can also maintain it manually by adding the customer number in the vendor general data.

3. Supplier Info Record

1. Maintain supplier info record with info type Subcontracting. The supplier info record will be used in the subcontracting service cost of the finished goods as well as automatic pricing in the purchase order.

Business Processes

After finalizing the configuration and defining the necessary master data for the "Sell from Subcontractor" cycle, these elements will form the foundation for demonstrating the end-to-end process of the scenario. The configuration ensures system settings align with the specific business requirements, while the master data—such as vendor details, subcontracting materials, and pricing conditions—supports accurate and seamless execution. Together, they illustrate the efficient handling of the scenario ensuring clarity and precision in each step of the cycle.

1. Create Sales Order - Subcontractor Fill-up

Create sales order using the Create Sales Order (VA01) application, add the supplier created in the Business Partner master data section. Moreover, add the material and the total quantity of finished goods that should be at the supplier's site.

2. At item level of the material add the External Subcontracting valuation type (ZEXTER-SC). Note, if the outbound delivery was created before adding the valuation type in the sales order, it can be added in the outbound delivery in the Goods Movement Data tab.

3. After displaying the sales order, the Schedule Line and Requirement Class created in the configuration section can be seen under the procurement tab.

4. In the schedule line tab, automatic Purchase Requisition was created after saving the sales order.

5. To view the sales order cost estimate, press on Menu, Extras, then press on Costing.

6. Under Qty. Struct., it can be seen the BOM Number used in the scenario based on the criteria mentioned in point 1(B) in Sales Order Cost Estimate configuration section, Production Version, Special Procurement Key that is coming from the material master. In step 4, these are the components and components quantity that make up the finished goods. Finally, in step 5, the system searches for the oldest active supplier info record based on material and plant.

7. As a result of the sales order cost estimate, condition type EK01 is updated after saving the document with the total value of the sales order cost estimate. Note, EK01 is a standard condition type.

2. Create Purchase Order

1. Create the purchase order, using Create Purchase Order (ME21N) application, with reference to the purchase requisition created by the sales order. Add storage location for the finished product in order to not added manually in the goods receipt.

3. Create Outbound Delivery - Raw Materials

1. Using the Subcontracting Monitor (Tcode ADSUBCON) application, select the purchase order and press on Delivery for PO.

2. Press on Create delivery.

3. Message shows that the outbound delivery has been created successfully.

4. Create Shipment & Shipment Completion (Goods Issue)

1. Go to Create Shipment (VT01N), add the below parameters, and press on Deliveries to add the outbound delivery created in the previous step.

2. After selecting all the statuses and pressing on Shpmt Completion and saving the document, the outbound delivery assigned to the shipment will be goods issued and that is because of the activity assigned to the shipment completion status mentioned in Goods Issue on Shipment Completion configuration section. No FI document is created for the material document generated from the outbound delivery.

3. If you go and check Stock at Subcontractor report, you can see that components are now in transit.

5. Post Goods Movement - Place in Storage

1. After the subcontractor contacts the company that the components have been received, go to Post Goods Movements (MIGO) and choose Place in Storage to add the material document generated from the previous point to state that the subcontractor has received the components, and the goods are now moved from in transit to unrestricted at subcontractor for usage. Movement type 30C to change stock from in transit to unrestricted use. No FI document is created here.

2. If you go and check Stock at Subcontractor report again, you can see that components have moved from in transit stock to unrestricted use stock.

6. Post Goods Movement - Goods Receipt

1. If your S/4HANA version is older than 2023 FPS02, you might face an error on goods receipt. To Avoid the error, implement SAP note 3421186.

2. The subcontractor shall contact the company to state the quantity of finished goods that have been manufactured, and the company should then receive this quantity. Go to Post Goods Movement (MIGO), choose Goods Receipt, and add the Purchase Order number. Stock should then be received on sales order stock that is created in Create Sales Order - Subcontractor Fill-up section.

3. The below accounting document generated from the goods receipt records the following. The debited inventory trading goods account means that the current value of the sales order stock is 200, which is the value coming from the Sales Order cost Estimate. If no valuation class is maintained in the material master in VC: Sales Order Stk in the material master Accounting 1, the G/L account is then taken from the normal valuation class. The GR/IR is then credited with 100 representing the amount to be paid to the supplier. The Subcontracting Services (Non-operating expense account) is debited with 100 to record the expense to the supplier. The Raw Materials Consumed (stock outward movement) are credited with 50 each against the debited Raw Material Inventory account with 50. Finally, the COGS trade (Non-operating expense account) represents the subcontracting expense and the consumption of raw materials and is credit with 200. Note, the actual COGS will be recorded in the consignment issue sales.

4. If you go to Stock - Multiple Materials report, you can see that the sales order stock for the created sales order has increased.

4. Create Outbound Delivery - Customer Consignment

1. To be able to use the finished goods in sales, outbound delivery for the Sales Order Subcontractor Fill-up has to be created and issued to decrease the sales order stock and increase the customer consignment stock. Go to Create Outbound Delivery - From Sales Orders application and create the outbound delivery. Press on Post Goods Issue and display the FI document from material document generated on the outbound delivery.

2. As seen below, the Inventory Trading Goods account is credited with 200, which is coming from the Inventory Trading Goods G/L account that of the goods receipt against the debited Inventory Trading Goods, G/L account that is of the normal valuation class with 200.

3. The previous accounting entry is a normal standard system behaviour, and that is because the valuation price of the sales order stock is separated from the valuation price of company's own stock. Therefore, in order to avoid price difference in the previous entry and for the consignment stock to have the same value of goods receipt, Split Valuation was needed as well as to Change the Price of Material for the external subcontracting valuation type in MR21. Check SAP note 212286, Price differences and exchange rate differences section, and SAP note 1915998.

4. If you go to Stock - Multiple Materials report, you can see that the customer consignment stock has increased after the goods issue for ZEXTER_SC valuation type.

4. Create Supplier Invoice & Outgoing Payment

1. Finally, create supplier invoice using the Create Supplier Invoice - Advanced (MIRO) application and payment for the supplier using the Post Outgoing Payment application.

Bonus

Case 1:

1. You can manually add your own costs, e.g. adding certain supplier info record or route, by using Unit Costing instead of Product Costing. To use Unit Costing, maintain the below parameters in the requirement class using the following path Sales and Distribution>Basic Functions>Account Assignment/Costing>Transfer of Requirements>Maintain Requirement Classes for Costing/Account Assignment.

2. When you create the sales order, choose Menu, Extras, then press on Costing.

3. Below are the item categories representing which type of costs can be added in the sales order costing. After adding the desired costs, in the same screen, go to Menu, Functions, Mark, and Set.

Case 2:

1. You can directly receive the raw material at subcontractor instead of own company by doing the following, create normal purchase order and in the Delivery Address tab add the Subcontractor Business Partner number and raise SC Supp flag.

2. Upon receiving the goods, the unrestricted quantity at the supplier's stock will increase.

Case 3:

1. To sell to a customer and at the same time deduct from the customer consignment of the subcontractor, create consignment issue sales order and add partner function SB Special Stk Partner and add the subcontractor.

Case 4:

1. If you create standard cost estimate, the supplier info record will be added automatically to the cost estimate because of the special procurement in the material master. To avoid this you will need to add the Production Version under the Qty. Struct. Tab. By doing this, Routes will be re-determined and Special Procurement will not be used in the cost estimate.

Case 5:

1. For repetitive manufacturing planned orders using the Confirm Repetitive Manufacturing (MFBF) application, the valuation type will be needed to be added Manually. Add the valuation type in the Receiving Batch field for the Internal Manufacturing valuation type.

Conclusion

The Sell from Subcontractor process is a critical component in managing the seamless integration of subcontracted goods and services into a company’s sales and distribution framework. This process relies on precise master data management, robust SAP configuration, real-time coordination, and strict compliance adherence. When executed effectively, it enhances operational efficiency, minimizes lead times and costs, and ensures customer satisfaction. By streamlining this process, organizations can achieve greater agility, strengthen supplier relationships, and drive sustainable business growth.

Resources

    1. 2865228 - Field(s) Missing in Business Partner Master Fiori Apps - SAP for Me
    2. 1915998 - Valuation of a Movement when Special Stock E/Q is involved - SAP ERP & SAP S/4 HANA - SAP ...
    3. 212286 - Overview Note: Valuation for goods movements - SAP for Me
    4. 2315671 - Delivery costs in a subcontracting scenario - SAP for Me
    5. 1780774 - M7347 "Enter valuation type" in VL02N - SAP for Me

entry.title: Germany – Batch upload Error and Solution
entry.content:
Introduction:

German batch upload can enter different phase of errors during the process. Its important to analyze the potential errors and process the upload.

Upload Error:

During the batch upload process in LSMW, following error pops up Source Structure and field mismatch while executing read data.

Error Analysis based on error description:

As per the error description, we will check source fields are content file sequence is matching.

If Fields are not matching, please check with the requestor.

If Fields are matching, we will move to the next step of analysis.

Step1:

German batch upload is processed in CSV format with Semi-colon. We have to check the file format is CSV.

We are uploading 2 files Header and item.

In the file received, we can clearly check the format above.

Header file in the example above is clearly the correct CSV format sent by the requestor. Contrastingly, Item file is not in the correct format (Microsoft excel worksheet).

In this case, we always send it back to the requestor to recheck and share the correct format for accuracy.

Step 2:

File format for both Header and Item file are accurate, but still, we get the same error.

Correct format:

Error:

In this case, we have to deep dive into the file and check the fields inside are aligned correctly with the upload format.

Header File Requestor format:

Batch upload Correct Format:

Format for header file is correctly matching the upload format. No issues observer here. If the format is mismatching send the correct sample format to the requestor for correction in the file.

Step 3:

We will check the item file field format to identify the potential formatting issue as header file has no error.

Item file format sent by the requestor:

Correct Batch Upload format:

In the comparison above, we can clearly see the format sent by the requestor is incorrect. Cells and values are separated by the requestor which is causing the error.  

Conclusion:

Based on 3 step analysis, it’s clear the file format and the value format sent by the requestor for item file is not accurate. Its important to understand requestors perspective and share the correct sample file to them as they may not expertise the upload process done by consultants. Once requestor reviews and sends the correct format. We are good to go for batch upload successfully.

entry.title: Concur Quick Expense v4 API
entry.content:
I am a Technical Consultant with SAP Concur and work with customers that wish to interface their data with Concur Expense and Concur Invoice. I regularly collaborate with customers that use APIs to manage the data exchange between their systems and the Expense solution provided by SAP Concur.

The Quick Expense API facilitates the creation of an expense entry by capturing essential details, including date, amount, and type of expense, with the option to attach a receipt image. This new entry can then be added to an expense report within Concur Expense, enabling the user to provide further specifics such as attendees and itemizations.

The Quick Expense API can be useful in many scenarios. Transactions can be created to aid in testing cycles, easing the burden on your testers to manually create expense entries. If you have a card program that is not able to be automatically imported into Concur from the card provider, you can automate the import of these transactions from information provided to you by the provider. The time savings from being able to programmatically create these transactions can be a significant savings for your organization.

Prerequisites

This post will not discuss authentication with Concur Expense. Please see this post to get an explanation on authentication and application building with Concur Expense.

You will need an application with the following SCOPEs.

Required Scopes:

  • quickexpense.writeonly          - Write quick expense
  • user.read                                - Get User Information, necessary for userID if using a user token
  • receipts.writeonly                   - Required if e-Bunsho is enabled

 

Optional Scope:

  • CONFIG                                 - Get Expense Configuration info, necessary for expenseTypeId

 

Works with these SAP Concur solutions:

  • Expense – Standard
  • Expense – Professional
  • Japan Market: If the partner is using this API to provide the e-Bunsho digital timestamp for the receipt, the partner should confirm that the client has e-Bunsho activated in SAP Concur

Introduction

The Quick Expense API will be discussed with examples for creating the transactions with and without an image. The images shown are tested using Opensource client API Bruno v.1.34.2. You may need to adjust the payload/configuration based upon the API client used.

Additional details regarding the Quick Expense v4 API can be found here.

Detailed Walkthrough

Determine the userID for the Quick Expense

The first step you should complete is to determine the userID that the transaction will be assigned. You can retrieve the profile of the user using this Profile API. This is the reason the user.read scope is listed as required for the Quick Expense API. This API could be used to determine the userID of the user making the profile API call.

GET     https://{datacenterURI}/profile/v1/me?schema=compact

If you wish to determine the userID for the given user by calling another Concur API, there are many to choose from that can be found in the Developer center documentation. These may require additional scopes to be added to the application. One example would be the use of the Identity v4.0 API.

Once the userID is determined, the expenseTypeId will need to be found.

Determine the expenseTypeId for the Quick Expense

The next step that must be completed is to determine the policies that the user is assigned. Transactions can only be created for expense types assigned to policies the user has been given. So, you must first determine the policies that the user has been assigned. Once you have identified the policies the user has been assigned, then you can determine the expense types available for the transaction which will be created by the Quick Expense API. If the Quick Expense API is not supplied an expense type, or a transaction is assigned an expense type that is not allowed for the user, the API will fail.

Determine the policies that are assigned for the user. The userID will be the value found in the last step.

GET     https://{datacenterURI}/expenseconfig/v4/users/{userID}/policies

Additional details regarding the Expense Configuration v4 APIs can be found here.

Example of the policy results for a given userID:

Next, determine the list of expense types available to a user based on a particular policy found in the previous API call. Based on the results, one of the expenseTypeId values will be used to create the transaction using the Quick Expense API.

GET     https://{datacenterURI}/expenseconfig/v4/users/{userID}/policies/{policyId}/expensetypes

Additional details regarding the Expense Configuration v4 APIs can be found here.

Example of the expense type results for a given userID and policyId:

Creating a Quick Expense without an Image

You have now determined the userID that the transaction will be assigned as well as the expenseTypeId. The template for this API call is listed below. Additional details can be found here.

Template for Quick Expense v4 API without an Image

POST   https://{datacenterURI}/quickexpense/v4/users/{userID}/context/{contextType}/quickexpenses

 

Name

Type

Description

userID

string

Required: The unique identifier of the SAP Concur user.

contextType

string

Required: The access level of the SAP Concur user, which determines the form fields they can view/modify. Supported value: TRAVELER.

You are now ready to create the payload for the Quick Expense.

First, determine the paymentTypeId that should be used for the quick expense.

Supported values:

  • CASHX  – Cash
  • CPAID   – Company Paid
  • PENDC – Pending Card Transaction

Next, create the body of the payload with the appropriate values that you wish to load for the user. I have an example payload below that you can use as a reference.

{
  "comment": "Team Lunch to Discuss Quick Expense",
  "expenseTypeId": "MEALS",
  "location": {
       "city": "Atlanta",
       "countryCode": "US",
       "countrySubDivisionCode": "US-GA"
  },
  "paymentTypeId": "CASHX",
  "transactionAmount": {
       "currencyCode": "USD",
       "value": 30.99
  },
  "transactionDate": "2024-12-27",
  "vendor": "Subway"
}

Then, call the Quick Expense v4 API with your payload.

Example of the results using the above payload:

This is what the expense will appear as in Concur.

There may be additional details required for the Concur user to enter once the expense is assigned to a report which is dependent upon the Concur configuration.

 

Creating a Quick Expense with an Image

As discussed in the previous section, you have now determined the userID that the transaction will be assigned as well as the expenseTypeId.

The template for this API call is listed below. Additional details can be found here.

Template for Quick Expense v4 API with an Image

POST     https://{datacenterURI}/quickexpense/v4/users/{userID}/context/{contextType}/quickexpenses/image

 

Name

Type

Description

userID

string

Required The unique identifier of the SAP Concur user.

contextType

string

Required The access level of the SAP Concur user, which determines the form fields they can view/modify. Supported values: TRAVELER.

fileContent

file

Required The quick expense image. Maximum size 50 MB. Supported image types are: PNG, PDF, TIFF, JPEG

Creating a quick expense with an image requires a multi-part form.

You can use the same format for the body as the previous example. A key value is added for the body titled “quickExpenseRequest”.

{
  "comment": "Team Lunch to Discuss Quick Expense",
  "expenseTypeId": "MEALS",
  "location": {
       "city": "New York",
       "countryCode": "US",
       "countrySubDivisionCode": "US-NY"
  },
  "paymentTypeId": "CASHX",
  "transactionAmount": {
       "currencyCode": "USD",
       "value": 33.87
  },
  "transactionDate": "2024-12-27",
  "vendor": "Good Restaurant"
}

A key value is also added for the body titled “fileContent”. The image that you wish to load should then be specified.

Example of the results using the above quickExpenseRequest payload and a fileContent image:

This is what the expense will appear as in Concur.

Conclusion

The Quick Expense API can easily be used to create expenses that can be assigned to a user. There can be significant time savings gained by using this API whether it is utilized to create test transactions or create transactions that could not be created by a card vendor.

Share and Connect

What do you think?  Do you have anything to add? Leave a comment below.

Did you find it useful? Give us a like and share on social media.

Want to know more about SAP Concur? Please follow here.

Want to ask questions about SAP Concur and its offerings? Ask here

Follow my profile for similar content.

Thank you!

entry.title: Customer Onboarding for SAP Service and Asset Manager
entry.content:
Are you a new SAP Service and Asset Manager Customer ready to start your project implementation?

To ensure a successful start to your SAP project, we’ve designed a Customer Onboarding Journey dedicated to SAP Service and Asset Manager.

Start your Onboarding Journey today by:

  1. Visiting the SAP Service and Asset Manager Onboarding Resource Center, your one-stop shop for a smooth onboarding process. Access ungated enablement resources, including guides on how to use SAP for Me, how to access your system, and useful information on what to expect and how to prepare for the entire project implementation.

  2. Registering for the Onboarding Webcasts, which are quarterly live sessions designed to get you up to speed with the onboarding process. Plan, register, and join these sessions to gain insights and clarify any questions you may have. If you can't join live, on-demand content is also available.

The 4 phases of your Onboarding Journey:

The content from the SAP Service and Asset Manager Onboarding Resource Center is organized into 4 phases:

This content is then delivered in 2 Onboarding Webcasts as follows:

Phase 1. Get Started with SAP 

The main objective is to make sure customers understand the SAP Service and Asset Manager system provisioning and access process and become familiar with the expected communications from SAP.

Additionally, we aim to make sure customers are aware of SAP channels, particularly SAP for Me, and know how to get support from SAP for both technical and non-technical issues.

Main topics covered:

  1. Get to know your onboarding journey
  2. Learn about SAP channels and s-user setup
  3. Get access to your system
  4. Get familiar with SAP Support

Phase 2. Plan your Implementation

The main objective is to provide the customer a high-level knowledge on how to better run, manage and accelerate their SAP Service and Asset Manager project implementation using the tools and resources included in their subscription.

Main topics covered:

  1. Get to know the key features of SAP Service and Asset Manager
  2. Learn about the offerings and channels that can support your implementation and after your go-live
  3. Run your project implementation with SAP Activate
  4. Learn about roles & system testing
  5. Get ready for final project tasks
  6. Keep innovating after go-live

In Conclusion:

The SAP Service and Asset Manager Onboarding Journey is meticulously designed to ensure that you’re fully prepared for a successful implementation, since contract signature to go-live.

Take the time to explore, plan, and engage with the resources at your disposal. The road to a successful go-live with SAP Service and Asset Manager starts here.

Kunal Bansal

entry.title: Re: MaxDB "No SQL connection possible despite ONLINE" but...
entry.content:
Hello JamesZ,

thanks for your response! I will see if this helps me in further analysis. Log area full was never the cause.

Anyway, we made some progress in this topic since I posted my issue here. Looks like issuing "dbmcli -U c" in the command line for a couple of times will lead to an "Error! Connection failed to node ... Connection broken to PID ..." every once in a while. I've also had the dbmcli crashing with a "Emergency stack trace". While seeing this behaviour it came to my attention that the installed anti virus (Eset) was scanning everything, especially files like "<SID>.dbm.shm".

Due to that we found the following SAP notes and asked the customer to exclude SAP concerning directories from scanning:
3356389 - Antivirus or other security software affecting SAP operations
3410068 - Exclusion of SAP related file\folders from virus scanning

At the moment we are waiting for feedback. Also changed the monitoring metric in Solution Manager to "best of 3", which means that Solution Manager will check three times within 30 minutes and if there was one positive (green) result, no alert will be raised.

Best
Daniel

entry.title: German xRechnung Template for SAP Business ByDesign
entry.content:
As of 1 January 2025, eInvoicing becomes the default method to issue invoices in Germany. SAP Business ByDesign already supports the exchange of electronic customer invoices and supplier invoices.      
More details on the sending of eInvoices (customer invoices) can be found in

  •  E-Invoicing with SAP Business ByDesign - SAP Community and
  •  Integrating SAP Business ByDesign to Peppol Networ... - SAP Community.    

SAP Business ByDesign supports three different options on how to receive e-invoices (supplier invoices).

  • Option 1: Use the standard SOAP service Manage Supplier Invoice In .
  • Option 2: Manual upload of SIV into SAP ByD leveraging transformation IDs.
  • Option 3: Automated upload of SIV into SAP ByD leveraging transformation IDs.

But for options 2 and 3 you need to maintain an XSLT file that translates the received einvoice into a format that is understandable for SAP Business ByDesign.     

Attached to this blog you will find an XSLT template that supports the Germand xRechnung format and can be used immediately.

IMPORTANT: You have to change the file type to .xslt before you can use it. 

DISCLAIMER: As this template is only an example it might be necessary to adapt the template to individual customer/supplier requirements.             
The following invoice fields are mapped in the XSLT template.


The fields marked with an asterix are mandatory fields for creating a simple supplier invoice in SAP Business ByDesign.

Take a look in this blog if you want to know how to maintain the XLT file in SAP Business ByDesign.

entry.title: Deployment Readiness: SAP Datasphere & SAP Analytics Cloud
entry.content:
Hello All and Happy New Year!

Beginning in February 2025, SAP Enterprise Support is offering two new pilot CQC service through the SAP Enterprise Support Advisory Council (ESAC) : CQC Deployment Readiness for both SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP Datasphere.
In essence, this service is intended to perform some configuration checks and ensure that customers are going live following SAP best practices.

Why: We want to ensure that customers can get started using both SAP Datasphere and SAP Analytics Cloud efficiently and effectively following SAP best practices, and identify potential risks, issues, or concerns, prior to the run phase. 

What: We offer a Deployment Readiness Check service (CQC) that provides customers with a pre go-live SAP best practices review for key settings, parameters, and general configuration guidance.

 

PREREQUISITES

For participation in the SAP Datasphere workstream you would need to have the following in place:

  • SAP Datasphere Production system with connectivity to/from Datasphere
  • Models within Datasphere
  • Analytical requirements complete
  • Go-live date determined
  • SAP access to the system and required Datasphere spaces provided to perform system checks


For participation in the SAP Analytics Cloud workstream you would need to have the following in place:

  • SAP Analytics Cloud Production system provisioned  with connectivity to relevant data sources
  • Go-live ready stories developed
  • Go-live date determined
  • Technical contact person available
  • System access for SAP provided

During the pilot service delivery an SAP service engineer would connect to your system, perform the relevant checks, and provide you with a detailed report of findings from the (SAP Analytics Cloud or SAP Datasphere) system with recommendations to any resolve critical issues. 

The intention is that via delivery of this service we can enable a smooth go-live, ensure avoidance of common pitfalls, and increase the speed of adoption of your end users.

Participation

This pilot is brought to you via the SAP Enterprise Support Advisory Council (ESAC).
To find out more about the SAP Enterprise Support Advisory Council in general visit the website. 
Details of this workstream will soon be included on that page.

To register you interest please contact us via email: esac@sap.com
Let us know your location / timezone, rough details of your project timeline, and state your interest in this pilot workstream.

 

Further Support and Empowerment via SAP Enterprise Support Value Maps

As always, you can compliment your experience by joining the SAP Enterprise Support Value Maps.
We are providing continued support and collaboration after the EGI workshops and new pilot workshop has completed, along with further enablement materials such as best practice documents and webinars.
This is a great way for you to get access to these services, along with further support and empowerment for SAP Data and Analytics solutions.

Join the SAP Analytics Solutions value map to receive further assistance, as well as access to a dedicated learning journey on SAP Data Warehousing topics.
* S user required

 

 

 

 

 

Quicklinks

  • SAP Datasphere Fundamentals: Expert Guided Implementation
  • E-Learning: SAP Datasphere Lifecycle Management
  • E-Learning: SAP Datasphere Security & Data Access Controls
  • Practical Guide: How to build SAP Analytics Cloud stories using SAP Datasphere
  • SAP Datasphere Connectivity Series

entry.title: Update of SAP Learning Journey on DevOps with SAP BTP
entry.content:
Happy to announce that the learning journey Discovering DevOps with SAP BTP has received a major overhaul that went live in SAP Learning end of December 2024. Besides applying the latest branding and style changes, updates of single services and their interplay have been considered.

Screenshot of learning journey

For example, concerning pipeline flexibility of SAP Continuous Integration and Delivery service, the new integration option of SAP Cloud Transport Management into SAP Cloud ALM, or the Generative AI assistant of SAP Automation Pilot service (described in a new lesson at the end of unit 3).

Example use case for GenAI assistant of SAP Automation Pilot

Although new content has been provided, we intentionally strived to keep the learning content on beginner level. Still, also more advanced leaners should be able to benefit from the update.

If you should be interested on how to make use of agile DevOps principles for your projects on SAP BTP, this would be the opportunity to get your jump start into the offering! You can go through the whole journey or only consume those lessons you are interested in. Give it a try here, free-of-charge.

entry.title: Re: Add item only for costing in production BOM
entry.content:
Hi @MySAPuser2002 ,

To assign a cost to a text item in the BOM:

Add Text Item to BOM: Use item category T (Text) in CS02 and enter a description (e.g., "In-house Liquid").

Assign Cost: In the BOM Costing tab, input a manual price for the text item.

Costing Variant Settings: Ensure the costing variant includes text items.

Run Cost Estimate: Use CK11N or check the process order cost analysis to confirm the liquid's value is included.

After creating the process order, check the cost analysis in the order via CO03 or KKS1 to confirm the liquid's cost is included.

Kindly upvote and accept if you found this helpful!

Thanks.

entry.title: Re: Inventory Accounting with Universal Parallel Accounting in SAP S/4HANA 2022
entry.content:
@WeekendVampire You cannot mix the classic approach with the settings in the currency and valuation profile and the new approach using universal parallel accounting in the same system. The Brazil country version hasn't been adjusted to be compatible with universal parallel accounting, so at the moment, if you are operating in Brazil, you will have to stay with the classic approach. 

Regards, Janet

entry.title: Elevate Your SAP Commerce Cloud Certification Journey with Our Certification Live Sessions
entry.content:
Abstract

In the ever-evolving world of SAP Commerce Cloud, achieving success means staying up-to-date with the latest features and insights. In response to the updated certification exams (2411) for business and technical users at the end of 2024, we are excited to offer two comprehensive certification preparation live sessions in Q1 2025. These sessions are designed to equip you with the tools and knowledge needed to excel in the crucial SAP Commerce Cloud certification exams.

Please be aware that any certification live session recordings you might encounter from before 2025 are outdated in relation to the current certification exams.

Live Sessions Overview

Live Session 1 (1 hour): Certification Prep for SAP Commerce Cloud – Business User

Purpose: This session is tailored to support participants preparing for the SAP Certified Application Associate - SAP Commerce Cloud Business User exam.

Certification Level: Associate

Target Audience: Business Consultants, Technology Consultants, Software Architects, and anyone interested in taking the certification exam.

Agenda:

  • Essential Topics Focus: Concentrate on key subjects from the Business User Certification Exam, emphasizing critical components that will be evaluated.
  • Strategic Preparation Guidance: Receive expert advice on crafting an effective study plan for associate-level success.

Live Session 2 (1 hour): Certification Prep for SAP Commerce Cloud - Developer

Purpose: This session is dedicated to empowering participants for the SAP Certified Development Professional - SAP Commerce Cloud Developer exam.

Certification Level: Professional

Target Audience: Software Developers, Software Architects, Technology Consultants, and anyone interested in taking the certification exam.

Agenda:

  • Key Topics Exploration: Dive into the main topics of the Developer Certification Exam, highlighting crucial elements that will be assessed.
  • Expert Preparation Tips: Gain insights into creating a robust study plan for professional-level certification success.

Common Elements Across Both Sessions

  • Comprehensive Coverage: Each session will systematically explore the exam topics, ensuring thorough understanding of essential components.
  • Interactive Learning: Engage with the content through interactive sessions, enriched by instructors' expertise and collective group insights.

Preferred Written Learning Materials?

If you prefer written guides, explore our study guides for both Business User and Developer exams to complement your preparation:

  • Study Guide for SAP Certified Associate - Business User - SAP Commerce Cloud Certification Exam 2411
  • Study Guide for SAP Certified Professional - Developer - SAP Commerce Cloud Certification Exam 2411

Already Certified?

If you've already achieved SAP Commerce Cloud certification in 2024 or before, remember to renew it by retaking the updated exam or participating in the stay certified programs. Look for email notifications from SAP about these programs and consider the following courses as preparation:

  1. Stay Certified – SAP Commerce Cloud Business User
  2. Stay Certified – SAP Commerce Cloud Developer

Conclusion

These upcoming live sessions in Q1 2025 are designed to be pivotal in your SAP Commerce Cloud certification journey. Whether you're a budding developer or a seasoned business user, expect in-depth coverage of essential exam topics and strategic preparation advice. This is about more than just passing exams - it's about becoming a leader in the SAP Commerce Cloud ecosystem.

Thanks for reading, and we look forward to seeing you at the sessions!

entry.title: AI Features to Enhance PMO Performance
entry.content:
How AI Features Can Improve PMO Performance

The Program Management Office (PMO) is a group or department that oversees the external workforce management program, handling and managing daily operations in the SAP Fieldglass application. The PMO is important for maintaining program efficiency and smooth technology operation.

AI Features That Can Help PMO Efficiency

SAP Fieldglass' AI enhances procurement, supporting PMOs in proactive staffing, risk mitigation, competitive pricing, and efficient workflow management. It helps to improve efficiency and decision-making in managing contingent and services workflows. Are you part of the PMO group in SAP Fieldglass? Let’s explore the available AI features and see how they can assist you.

AI-Assisted Job Description Creation

SAP Fieldglass offers an AI job description tool that creates and enhances job postings using generative AI. This tool lets you input job title, description, and required skills for relevant results.

See the Required Setup to use AI for job posting descriptions.

AI-Assisted Job Description Translation

SAP Fieldglass auto-translates job postings using AI, reducing manual effort, standardizing processes, and supporting global expansion.

See the Required Setup to use AI for job posting description translation.

Usage of AI to generate and translate job posting descriptions:         

AI-Assisted SOW Description Creation

The AI-Assisted SOW Description feature helps you to create a statement of work description based on various elements of the SOW.

AI in SOW creation reduces the back-and-forth communication with suppliers. Simple data input is needed to generate comprehensive SOW descriptions, enhancing clarity for suppliers and accelerating collaboration.

See the Required Setup to use AI for generating SOW description creation.

Usage of AI to generate SOW description:             

Using Machine Learning Features for Candidate Analysis

Machine Learning helps hiring managers and PMOs find the best candidates by evaluating their experience, skills, and resumes. It helps keeping the hiring decisions fair and impartial.

Key features include:

Resume Assessment

Resume assessment scores with job descriptions, removing personal information for fair and unbiased results.

See the Required Setup to use the Resume Assessment feature.

Experience Assessment

The Experience Assessment evaluates candidates’ fit with job requirements, providing Related Experience (percentage that indicates how well their experience matches the job requirements) and Industry Match (keyword comparison that indicates if there's a strong match).

See the Required Setup to use the Experience Assessment feature.

Highlighting Skills

The Highlighting Skills auto-detects and highlights key skills on resumes, saving time and providing quick assessment of candidate strengths and fit.

See the Required Setup to use the Highlighting Skills feature.

 

ℹ️  The Generative AI features are available for you if you have the RISE Premium Edition or the RISE Premium Plus Edition package.

See Also 

Company Configuration: AI and Machine Learning Features

entry.title: Customer Onboarding for SAP Field Service Management
entry.content:
Are you a new SAP Field Service Management Customer ready to start your project implementation?

To ensure a successful start to your SAP project, we’ve designed a Customer Onboarding Journey dedicated to SAP Field Service Management.

Start your Onboarding Journey today by:

  1. Visiting the SAP Field Service Management Onboarding Resource Center, your one-stop shop for a smooth onboarding process. Access ungated enablement resources, including guides on how to use SAP for Me, how to access your system, and useful information on what to expect and how to prepare for the entire project implementation.

  2. Registering for the Onboarding Webcasts, which are quarterly live sessions designed to get you up to speed with the onboarding process. Plan, register, and join these sessions to gain insights and clarify any questions you may have. If you can't join live, on-demand content is also available.

The 4 phases of your Onboarding Journey:

The content from the SAP Field Service Management Onboarding Resource Center and Onboarding Webcasts is organized into 4 phases:

 

This is the content in more detail:

Phase 1. Get Started with SAP 

The main objective is to make sure customers understand the SAP Field Service Management system provisioning and access process and become familiar with the expected communications from SAP.

Additionally, we aim to make sure customers are aware of SAP channels, particularly SAP for Me, and know how to get support from SAP for both technical and non-technical issues.

Main topics covered:

  1. Get to know your onboarding journey
  2. Learn about SAP channels and s-user setup
  3. Get access to your system
  4. Get familiar with SAP Support

Phase 2. Plan your Implementation

The main objective is to provide the customer a high-level knowledge on how to better run, manage and accelerate their SAP Field Service Management project implementation using the tools and resources included in their subscription.

Main topics covered:

  1. Run your project implementation with SAP Activate
  2. Manage your project with SAP Cloud ALM
  3. Learn about the offerings and channels that can support your implementation
  4. Accelerate your project with SAP Best Practices
  5. Learn about roles & system testing

Phase 3. Get Enabled

The main objective is to present the customer with free learning and enablement resources to help them effectively use the SAP Field Service Management solution.

Main topics covered:

  1. Get to know the key features of SAP Field Service Management
  2. Learn about the offerings and channels that can support you with having a deeper knowledge of your solution 
  3. Check how you can interact with SAP Experts and Peers

Phase 4. Prepare to Go Live

The main objective is to ensure a smooth go-live and prepare customers to run their business effectively.

Main topics covered:

  1. Get ready for final project tasks
  2. Learn about the offerings and channels that can support you after your go-live
  3. Keep innovating after go-live

In Conclusion:

The SAP Field Service Management Onboarding Journey is meticulously designed to ensure that you’re fully prepared for a successful implementation, since contract signature to go-live.

Take the time to explore, plan, and engage with the resources at your disposal. The road to a successful go-live with SAP Field Service Management starts here.

entry.title: Re: Installing Composable Storefront for SAP Commerce 2211.20
entry.content: Hi @Rakesh_kp . It appears you're talking to the wrong server, and you need to take a look at the .npmrc file in the current directory. That's where the npm command (also the ng command) looks to know what repository to query. You need to query the SAP Repository-Based Shipment Channel (RBSC), not the public open source repository. That file also needs to contain valid NPM Base64 Credentials (which you generated as part of these instructions). Also, check that your user has a license for Spartacus.

entry.title: Why do we need SAP Asset Performance Management (SAP APM)?
entry.content:
Everything evolves, and maintenance is no exception- continuously evolving into a more streamlined and efficient process!

With evolving technology, the maintenance practices have improved as well. With the advent of Industry 4.0 and availability of IoT there are new avenues for maintenance as well, which are cost effective and increases the reliability of machines. Data science, AI and ML can be used to predict and avoid failures based on historical data.

Maintenance always has budget constraints and I’m yet to see an industry where maintenance has unlimited resources to spend and keep the plant running without issues. Improved maintenance processes can save a lot of money and resources, while also increasing the reliability and predictability of the machines.

Historical Maintenance Process:

Historically, the only maintenance that we used to do was Reactive maintenance, which means you maintain something only when it breaks. This could be either corrective or breakdown in nature. The problem with this kind of maintenance is it is very costly and can cause long breakdowns where a line, a section or the whole plan is inoperative.

The reason Reactive maintenance is expensive has 3 major factors:

  1. Possible loss of production.
  2. Unplanned human resource requirements.
  3. Unplanned spare part requirements.

 

Current Maintenance Processes:

Apart from the historical Reactive maintenance, the maintenance practices slowly evolved into Proactive maintenance, which means doing repetitive maintenance tasks for instance changing certain spare parts of a machine such as gaskets, o-rings or changing the lubricating oil on a regular basis. The schedule or interval could be suggested by the OEMs or could be based on the experience of the operator. This was effective and helped avoid breakdowns. However, this was still expensive as the parts were being changed even when the parts are in good condition. This also might have environmental impact due to wastage of parts and oil. Proactive maintenance is still the most prevalent maintenance strategy in most industries.

The reason Proactive maintenance is expensive has 2 major factors:

  1. Excessive spare part consumption.
  2. Increased human resource requirements.

Benefits of Proactive maintenance:

  1. Increased reliability.
  2. Reduced unplanned shutdowns.

 

Future Maintenance Processes using SAP APM:

  • ­The next evolution in maintenance is Predictive and Prescriptive maintenance.

Predictive maintenance is a way to predict the future failures so that timely actions could be taken to plan and handle the failure. Prescriptive maintenance though on top of predicting the failure, also prescribes actions that can be taken to avoid that future failure.

For example, if the RPM of a purifier is lower than normal, then using SAP APM’s Asset Health module an Alert or a Notification could be generated to inspect the purifier.

  • Another evolution in maintenance is RCM- Reliability Centered Maintenance.

RCM dictates identifying critical assets/ machines, defining failure modes based on the operating context and creating a strategy for the maintenance of identified machines.

For example, we identify a fuel oil purifier as a critical piece of equipment since it supplies fuel oil to the main engine. We define operating context as heavy oil as the medium the purifier is processing. Then we define the maintenance strategies on it based on the experience and history of purifiers with the same operating context (fuel oil). Maintenance strategies can be defined in the form of reactive, proactive or improvement recommendations. The recommendations could be converted into time or counter based (for ex. run hours) maintenance plans and task lists. These strategies could be revisited based on the feedback and thus can be improved continuously.

This could also aid in predictive maintenance. For instance, while working on the notification created in the example under predictive/ prescriptive maintenance and converting it into a Work Order a task list could be included based on the Recommendations from SAP APM’s Asset Reliability Engineering module.

Benefits of Predictive maintenance:

  1. Increased reliability.
  2. Reduced maintenance cost.
  3. Consistently improving maintenance program.

 

So, to answer the original question which a lot of prospective customers’ would be asking, “why do we need SAP Asset Performance Management (APM)” -  SAP APM with it’s Asset Health and Asset Reliability Engineering modules can help industries transition from the traditional reactive & proactive maintenance to predictive, prescriptive maintenance and RCM. This will help in overall improvement of maintenance processes, increase in reliability and reduction in maintenance costs.

You can also read my other blogs:

What is SAP Asset Performance Management?

SAP APM in Maritime Industry

entry.title: Can I overlap production orders? (Not operations)
entry.content:
Good Afternoon,

Is there a way, from a capacity planning perspective, to overlap production orders?

At the moment this is what appears to happen:

Part A on Prod Ord 1 for qty 10, takes 5 days to make, and is required to make Part B.

Part B on Prod Ord 2 for qty 10 takes 10 days to make.

SAP will say the process to make 10x Part B (including the Part A sub assembly) will take 15 days

What I want to see is SAP telling me that instead of 15 days, this would take 10.5 days, because after the first half day I would have completed 1 Part A, which then allows be to start building part B.

Is this at all possible??

entry.title: Re: API REST integration (new UI)
entry.content:
Hello there, 

Just wanted to jump also to this topic as the new UI was causing me some headache as well when it came out. I didn't bother too much with it, rather just duplicated some old REST API resources, and could keep on with those. 

But I did find some pretty good use of it in finally being able to implement `PATCH` requests to a Xano backend. 

Some useful thoughts could potentially be: 
1. for simple query parameter setting you can use the `APPEND_URL_PARAMETERS()` formula to easily combine an object or variable into a GET query string. 

2. to ensure that your backend (in our case Xano) can identify the content of a POST/PUT/PATCH request, we found it useful to also wrap the request body into an `ENCODE_JSON()` formula. 

3. this is something I plan on exploring a little bit more: it seems that this new UI also enables the REST api to be used with Data Components (even including pagination)

I honestly hope that the UI can get slightly better and there can be more documentation about this kind of implementation, but until then this page helped me a lot: 
SAP BTP REST direct integration as it seems that the new universal REST API integration is just an SAP BTP REST integration, without predefined routes. Also I presume that as we can slowly notice other significant UI changes, I presume that the REST api integration also goes into this direction: SAP Build Help centre

entry.title: Re: The Required Functional modules Knowledge.
entry.content:
Hi @Sadory 

In general, there are four Modules that have repeated during my developer career: Material Management, Procurement, Sales and Distribution and Finance.

For MM: I suggest to have an overall understanding of Materials, Also understand the terms Plants and Storage Locations.

For Procurement: Have an overall understanding of Purchase Orders, Inbound Deliveries and Service Entry Sheets.

For SD: Have an overall understanding of Sales Orders, Outbound Deliveries and Invoices. Also understand the terms Sales Organization and Distribution Channel.

For FI: Have an overall understanding of Billing Documents, Payments and Validations/Substitutions. Also understand the terms Company Code, Account, Cost Center.

Of course, every module is made of tons of other terms, but in my case, understanding the terms I mentioned above helped me to better communicate with Functional Consultants.

entry.title: Re: MDG DQM Validation Rules – Access additional data model tables without ABAP coding
entry.content:
Hello,

it is important to note that the change request and the process differ in the fields that are filled.

The components Process ID, Step Number and Source System are only filled if the processing takes place within a process. This is the case with the Evaluate Data Quality process.

The Source ID is filled in both the change request and the process. As can be seen in your screenshots, it is filled in both cases. With the change request, the primary key of the object currently being processed is filled here. For the BP, this is the BP number.

Best regards
Stefan Weingand

entry.title: Re: Anticipo Proveedor con OC de Activo Fijo
entry.content:
Hi SFerrand,

Unfortunately,  it is impossible to post a downpayment with a purchase order associated with a Fixed asset in the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

You can create an Investment Project (WBS) and then the purchase order with the AccClassCategory Projects.  

In the purchase order, you can add the values of the downpayment

And create the downpayment request with the Monitor Purchase Order Down Payments app

You must run the whole purchase order cycle and settle the project to the final fixed asset.

SAP Note 3154459 3154459 - AA834 / F5378 / SFIN_FI017 when posting a downpayment to an asset SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

Best regards

 

Edna

entry.title: Re: UKM_BP error R1 206 when BP Role is not the standard...
entry.content:
Read 3502521 - Error in transaction UKM_BP: "BP role UKM000 does not exist" (message number: R1206) 

Basically this role is hard-coded in the transaction 😒 actually you are calling BP with some hidden parameter id in memory.

 

REPORT  ukm_bp_start.

PARAMETERS p_id TYPE bu_partner NO-DISPLAY.

DATA:
  lc_role    LIKE bus_joel_main-partner_role VALUE 'UKM000',
  tcod       LIKE sy-tcode VALUE 'BP',
  lv_partner TYPE bu_partner.

 

Did you try to create a Z-transaction changing the default value for role in the wrapper report ?

entry.title: SAP-CRM V7.13 (non-HANA): Divide data into 2 clients to support a carve out
entry.content: Currently one client (100) consists of data of 2 divisions. That should change: Every division should become an own client (Client 100 and 200 on the same system). I have the idea to simply create a second Client 200 and go through all datatables (Masterdata and transactions), starting with BUT000 and COMM_PRODUCT and simply change the Clientnumber to 200 for the data of the carved out division via SQL? It appears to be just a minor effort to cascade down from BUT000 and COMM_PRODUCT to related masterdatatables and transactiontables. The result would be that within our database-tables we would have data of the 2 clients. Are there any concerns of doing that? To me it appears the simplest way, just simple updates via DBACockpit, no deletes. I have the impression that consulting companies and also SAP are not recommending that and instead prefer downloads and uploads, sometime via Excel, which leads to serious issues getting the correct state of transactions and moving history data. I also see some challenges related to the datamodel, like CRMD_LINK... and the integration to ERP, but that is far less critical to me than any other approach. Does anyone have experience with that approach?

entry.title: Alternate resources (via WC classification)not creating multiple modes in PDS
entry.content:
Dear Experts, 

We are trying to create the possibility to re-schedule production orders on alternate resources in ePPDS via WC classification. Following are the steps done but the PDS do not have multiple modes for each alternate WC. 

1. WCs are classified via a 019 classification 

2. All WCs are CIF'ed 

3. BoM, Routing (Standard WC), PV created and CIF'ed 

4. Only one mode is visible in the PDS. 

We looked at the validity dates of the characteristic, classification, routings etc. and still cannot identify the root-cause. 

 

Looking forward to read your opinions and advice on how to set this up in the system.

 

Thanks,

Sandeep

entry.title: Tenant and domain in SAP Analytic Cloud
entry.content:
Hello everyone, I have a question about SAP Analytics Cloud versions. For one client, I have the information summarized in the attached screenshot "ClientWithDifferentTenant.png," where the SAC tenant has the following domain: "https://******q.eu10.analytics.cloud.sap" Meanwhile, for most other clients, I have the configuration shown in the screenshot "ClientHCSTenant.png," and the tenant has the following domain: "https://******.eu10.hcs.cloud.sap"

Could you please explain the difference between these two SAC versions? Are there any limitations associated with the "analytics.cloud" tenant?

Thanks

Dino

entry.title: Re: Filtering table measures with a dropdown list
entry.content:
Hi @Rick05,

I tried the same scenario filter measures in table I have below measure's as shown in table 

2. I used below script on dropdown.

var selectedMeasure = Dropdown_3.getSelectedKey();
if(selectedMeasure ==="Quantity"){
Table_5.getDataSource().setDimensionFilter(Alias.MeasureDimension,["Quantity"]);

}else{

Table_5.getDataSource().setDimensionFilter(Alias.MeasureDimension,["Discount"])
;
Table_5.getDataSource().setDimensionFilter(Alias.MeasureDimension,["Profit"]);
Table_5.getDataSource().setDimensionFilter(Alias.MeasureDimension,["Sale"]);

3. Its filter according to measures selection

4. If I select Sale it will filter as per Sale's Measure 

Kindly upvote if you find it helpful!

Thanks
